title: spergularia borealis
intro: Spergularia borealis, also known as the sand spurry or northern sandwort, is a low-growing, annual plant species native to Europe and North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Spergularia borealis include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and support immune function. Herbal preparations of Spergularia borealis, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, are used to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
